justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (task_context says 'Sprint 2 is explicitly deferred — do NOT implement any retry logic'). Domain rules cover only Sprint 1 rules (HTTP POST, 2xx = delivered, 5s timeout). All three required ACs present (AC1, AC2, AC3) plus a bonus AC4 for timeout. 5-second timeout is included in domain rules and architecture guidance. Sprint 2 content is acknowledged and explicitly excluded with a dedicated architecture_guidance entry stating 'Do NOT implement WebhookRetryScheduler, BullMQ job queue, or any backoff logic.' MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as a design note with clear instruction to leave a no-op call site with a comment. No retry logic is included in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Sprint 1/Sprint 2 boundary is exceptionally clear — both in task_context and in a dedicated architecture_guidance entry explicitly forbidding retry implementation
AC4 (timeout → failed) was intelligently inferred from the 5-second domain rule and transparently documented as an assumption
MJ's extension points guidance is faithfully passed through with practical implementation advice (no-op call site, comment marking the hook point)
Architecture guidance explicitly states retry code must only exist as comments/TODOs, never executable logic
Terminology alignment section adds clarity for Shaq on domain-to-code mapping
Open questions are thoughtful and actionable — event schema and persistence are genuine gaps
